title: Pakistan Birth Certificate Guide – Union Council & NADRA Wizard (2026 Updated)
legal_basis: Births and Deaths Registration Act + NADRA Ordinance 2000 + Provincial CRVS Laws (2026). First step always: Union Council Computerized Birth Registration Certificate (CBRC). Punjab: Free registration & computerized certificate up to 7 years late (CM policy 2026). NADRA CRC fees: Normal Rs 50, Urgent/Executive Rs 500.

wizard.title: Birth Registration Wizard
wizard.subtitle: Union Council CBRC & NADRA B-Form • 2026 Verified

whats_this.heading: Birth Certificate Wizard
whats_this.description: Obtaining a birth certificate is a two-step process in Pakistan: Union Council Computerized registration (CBRC) followed by NADRA B-Form (CRC). This 2026-updated wizard reflects current biometric rules and provincial policies.
quick_instructions: Union Council registration (CBRC) is mandatory before applying for a NADRA B-Form/CRC.
Punjab residents enjoy free registration for up to 7 years late (no court order needed).
Biometric requirements vary by age: 0-3 years (None), 3-10 years (Photo), 10-18 years (Full fingerprints/Iris).
whats_this.disclaimer: General guidance based on 2026 rules. Fees and requirements vary by province. Not legal advice.

document_need.title: What do you require regarding birth certificates?
document_need.description: Select the option that best describes your current need.

options.title: New Registration
options.description: First-time CBRC registration at Union Council or NADRA B-Form issuance.

options.title: Correction
options.description: Fixing name, DOB, or parent spelling errors in existing records.

options.title: Replacement / Duplicate
options.description: Reissuing certificates that were lost or damaged.

onsitePhases.title: Gather Evidence
onsitePhases.description: Collect mandatory proofs such as Parents' CNICs, Nikah Nama Copy, and Hospital Slip/Vaccination Card before visiting any office.

onsitePhases.title: Union Council (CBRC)
onsitePhases.description: Submit documents to Secretary UC to obtain the Computerized Birth Registration Certificate. In Punjab/Sindh, check the Pak-ID app option for online processing.

onsitePhases.title: NADRA B-Form
onsitePhases.description: Present UC CBRC at the NADRA counter. Biometrics will be captured based on age. Pay normal fee Rs 50 or Urgent Rs 500.

onsitePhases.title: Final Collection
onsitePhases.description: Track status via SMS or 1777 Helpline. Pick up the final computerized B-Form or CNIC from the center counter.

onlinePhases.title: Pak-ID Portal Account Registration
onlinePhases.description: Create an account on the Pak-ID portal. This option is primarily available for UC registration in Punjab and Karachi districts.

onlinePhases.title: Application Submission & Document Upload
onlinePhases.description: Fill out the birth registration details and upload digital copies of parent CNICs, Nikah Nama, and Hospital proofs.

onlinePhases.title: Verification & Fee Payment
onlinePhases.description: Pay any applicable local fees safely via the online platform interface.

onlinePhases.title: Delivery / Print
onlinePhases.description: Receive the digital certificate in the secure vault or via courier depending on your selected options.

documents_checklist.label: Parents' CNIC/NICOP
documents_checklist.description: Must be valid and unexpired (Both preferred)

documents_checklist.label: Hospital Slip / Vaccination Card
documents_checklist.description: Strong proof of birth date and location

documents_checklist.label: 2 Witness CNIC copies
documents_checklist.description: Required primarily for Home Births along with Affidavit

validation.title: Certificate Validation
validation.description: Ensure your Birth Certificate meets all legal and immigration requirements before usage

upload_section.title: Upload Your Birth Certificate
upload_section.description: Upload your final Birth Certificate to Document Vault

items.label: Child's name spelling matches passport and other documents exactly
Father's and mother's names matches their CNIC spellings
Date of Birth is accurate and matches vaccination/hospital records
Place of birth (City/District) is accurately captured
Certificate has official Union Council / NADRA seal and signature
Date of issuance is clearly visible
QR code or tracking number is present

tips: Union Council CBRC is mandatory before NADRA B-Form application.
Punjab: Registration is free up to 7 years late (CM policy 2026).
pitfalls: Applying at NADRA center without a Computerized UC Certificate (CBRC).

tips: Urgent/Executive B-Form (Rs 500) is usually ready in 3-5 days.
Normal B-Form fee is only Rs 50.
pitfalls: Spelling errors in initial UC registration are hard to fix later.

tips: Verify all spellings match your passport and other official documents exactly.
Check that the NADRA seal and signature are clearly visible on the certificate.
pitfalls: Don't assume the certificate is correct without verifying every detail.
Missing barcode/QR code can invalidate the document internationally.
